test.name: Simple place lookup — Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
test.description: Basic Wikipedia lookup for a genealogically relevant place. The simplest happy path — single-word topic, no apostrophes or parentheses, clear Wikipedia article exists. Tests the core workflow: call wikipedia_search, fill the template, save the file with a slug-normalized filename.

input.user_message: Look up Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania on Wikipedia

additional_criteria: Should call `wikipedia_search` with a query targeting the place (the exact form like 'Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania' or 'Schuylkill County' matters less than that it would retrieve the intended article)

Should save the summary to a file in the user's working folder — using `Write` to a markdown file is the production behavior. Displaying the result in chat without saving misses the 'save' part of the skill's purpose

The saved filename should slug-normalize the article title: 'Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania' → 'schuylkill-county-pennsylvania.md' (the comma + space collapses to a single hyphen; no leading/trailing hyphens)
The saved file's content should follow the template (article title as H1, the full extract, the source URL) — no fabricated content beyond what the tool returned
